text:
- #2809 ADR-0075 amendment: diagnostic surface is not app surface — propose nmp-devtools — the core proposal for the X-Ray/devtools diagnostic surface; open, `status:needs-adr` [...] **NMP#2809** — OPEN. Labels: priority:p2, category:decision, status:needs-adr, area:architecture. Proposes amending ADR-0075 to split "app surface" (unchanged: no Trellis vocabulary crosses UniFFI/web) from a new "diagnostic surface": a dev-build-only `nmp-devtools` crate exposing Trellis trace/audit data via an MCP server (`why_subscription_open`, `why_view_stale`, `what_closed_this_relay`, `list_scope_inventory`, `replay_session`) plus redacted trace export for bug reports. Explicitly excludes metrics/dashboards/log aggregation — receipts only. Names four upstream trellis prerequisites: #114 (serializable traces), #125 (audit retention toggle), #122 (typed resource keys), #126 (scope reclamation) — **all four now closed**. Sequenced after NMP #2808 (shadow→authority promotion), which is also now closed/merged (PR #2832, "Promote Trellis feed-session reconciliation to authority," merged today, 2026-07-03). Every stated prerequisite is done; #2809 is unblocked, just needs the ADR decision. No comments. [...] Assistant: The survey confirms: no epic exists, every upstream prerequisite is closed (NMP #2808 authority promotion merged **today** via #2832), and Trellis already has a trace-replay UI (Flight Recorder workstation, trellis#160) that the time-travel phase can reuse. I'll create the epic in `nostr-multi-platform` — that's where 4 of the 5 workstream items and the ADR live, and where the predecessor epic (#2626) lived. Checking its labels first.

Assistant: **Epic created: [nostr-multi-platform#2858](https://github.com/pablof7z/nostr-multi-platform/issues/2858)** — no epic existed before (the surveyor confirmed nothing ties this workstream together; the closest ancestors, NMP #2626/#2627, are closed). I cross-linked it from chirp#30 and NMP#2809, and yes: **time travel is in the plan as committed scope (Phase E), not a stretch goal** — per your message, it's written up as the recorded/replayable debugging capsule with scrubbing, not log viewing. [...] **The plan (five phases in the epic):**

- **Phase A — ADR-0075 amendment** (#2809): define "diagnostic surface" vs "app surface," and update doctrine-lint plus the Trellis public-API leak ratchet (#2642) so the exemption is enforced mechanically, not case-by-case.
- **Phase B — nmp-devtools stream**: ordered open/replace/refresh/close events with owner/provenance/revision/refcount/teardown data; join Trellis `ResourcePlan` → `ApplyDependentInterestDelta` outcomes into bounded retained records (today they're test-only traces); correlate with the existing relay diagnostics; and define the **NMP-owned receipt shape** so Chirp never sees `ResourcePlan`/`ResourceKey`/`Graph`.
- **Phase C — agent-first prover (your CLI/REPL/TUI ask)**: the MCP server (`why_subscription_open`, `why_view_stale`, `what_closed_this_relay`, `list_scope_inventory`, `replay_session`) plus a headless CLI that drives a real Chirp session against real relays and also loads/scrubs exported capsules. Exit criterion baked into the epic: *an agent can answer "why is my feed empty?" from receipts alone.* This lands **before** the pane, so agents can iterate on the diagnostic itself.
- **Phase D — Chirp X-Ray pane** (chirp#30 v1): dev-build-only declared projection/devtools channel; Chirp renders, never parses.
- **Phase E — time travel** (chirp#30 v2): dev-only recorder, ring-buffer retention with explicit export, redaction policy with local/debug vs shareable modes, correlation persisted into the capsule so scrubbing reconstructs full cause chains (refcount 2→1 vs 1→0→CLOSE), and replay-from-capsule-alone so a redacted trace attached to an issue is debuggable without the device or live relays.

One reuse note the surveyor surfaced: Trellis's **Flight Recorder workstation** (trellis#160, merged today) is already a full trace-replay UI — provenance, transaction timeline, receipt inspector, replay tabs. Phase E should feed NMP capsules into it for desktop-side inspection rather than building a second scrubber; the epic says so.

Nothing was implemented — the deliverables are the epic, the two cross-link comments, and this plan. Natural next step when you're ready: draft the ADR-0075 amendment text for #2809, since it's the decision gate everything else queues behind.
